result:
Done. All three operations are implemented in `CsvRow.calr`, the project builds cleanly, and behavior is verified end-to-end (2 of the 10 build cycles used).

What I added to the existing `NeedsQuoting` module, following its conventions (pure `В§E{}` effects, prefix s-expressions):

- **`EscapeField`** (`f002`) вҖ” calls `NeedsQuoting`; if true, wraps the field in double-quotes with internal quotes doubled via `(replace field "\"" "\"\"")`, otherwise returns it verbatim.
- **`JoinRow`** (`f003`) вҖ” takes `List<str>`, escapes each field and joins with commas using a first-element flag; the empty list yields the empty string.
- **`SplitRow`** (`f004`) вҖ” a while-loop state machine over the line with an in-quotes flag: doubled quotes inside a quoted field emit one literal quote, commas/CR/LF inside quotes are content, a comma outside quotes ends the field, and the accumulated field is always pushed at end-of-line (so `""` parses to one empty field and a trailing comma yields a trailing empty field). It builds a `List<str>` with `В§PUSH`, so it declares `В§E{mut}`.

Two things worth noting:

- The first build failed because I wrote the list type as `[str]`, which Calor maps to `string[]` rather than `List<string>`. I confirmed in the compiler's `TypeMapper` that generic types pass through (`List<str>` вҶ’ `List<string>`, matching the pinned public surface) and fixed both signatures.
- I verified behavior with a throwaway console project *outside* the source tree (since test files aren't allowed in it): all 14 targeted cases passed вҖ” quoting, escaping, empty-list/empty-line edge cases, trailing commas, embedded CR/LF вҖ” plus a 20,000-row randomized `SplitRow(JoinRow(fields))` round-trip. The scratch project has been deleted.

The generated C# surface is `CsvRow.CsvRowModule` with static `NeedsQuoting(string)вҶ’bool`, `EscapeField(string)вҶ’string`, `JoinRow(List<string>)вҶ’string`, `SplitRow(string)вҶ’List<string>`, as the spec pins.
